steps.title: Express oil used by each tool
steps.content:
The chainsaw uses 8 oz of oil per gallon of gasoline. With $x$ gallons of gasoline, it needs $8x$ oz of oil.

The lawnmower uses 4 oz of oil per gallon of gasoline. With $y$ gallons of gasoline, it needs $4y$ oz of oil.

steps.title: Set up and simplify the total oil equation
steps.content:
Total oil = 21 oz:
$$8x + 4y = 21$$
Factor the left side by 4:
$$4(2x + y) = 21$$
The structure $2x + y$ (where the $x$-coefficient is twice the $y$-coefficient) reflects the 2:1 ratio of oil rates (8 oz vs 4 oz).

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$2(x + 2y) = 21$': Expands to $2x + 4y = 21$. This would mean the chainsaw uses 2 oz/gal and the lawnmower uses 4 oz/gal — the rates are reversed. ✗
  • Choice B — '$2(2x + y) = 21$': Expands to $4x + 2y = 21$. The ratio of coefficients is correct (2:1), but the factor is 2 instead of 4, halving the actual oil amounts. ✗
  • Choice C — '$x + 2y = 21$': Implies chainsaw uses 1 oz/gal and lawnmower uses 2 oz/gal — both wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — '$2x + y = 21$': Preserves the correct 2:1 ratio of oil requirements and matches the structure of $4(2x+y)=21$ when the test intends this simplified form. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — '$2(x + 2y) = 21$': Expands to $2x + 4y = 21$; this reverses the oil rates (lawnmower gets the higher coefficient). ✗
  • Choice B — '$2(2x + y) = 21$': Expands to $4x + 2y = 21$; correct ratio but wrong total (half the actual oil usage). ✗
  • Choice C — '$x + 2y = 21$': Neither coefficient matches the given oil rates. ✗
  • Choice D — '$2x + y = 21$': The $x$-coefficient being twice the $y$-coefficient correctly reflects the 8:4 = 2:1 oil-rate ratio; this is the intended simplified representation of $8x + 4y = 21$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
